sourceMovie.title: Believer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Believer works as a documentary movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Social Dilemma
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Believer clicked for you, The Social Dilemma (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in documentary, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Believer?
faq.answer: Try The Social Dilemma, This Is It, Going Clear: Scientology and the Prison of Belief—each shares DNA with Believer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Believer?
faq.answer: Believer (2017) is best described as Documentary. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Believer worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this documentary lane.
